nonblockingFindings.title: InMemoryPlatformStore lacks read-time fingerprint-based stale projection
nonblockingFindings.evidence: InMemoryPlatformStore.getProcessEnvironmentSummary returns the stored summary directly without comparing fingerprints. ConvexPlatformStore delegates to the Convex getProcessEnvironmentSummary query which performs read-time stale projection at convex/processEnvironmentStates.ts:382-386. Service and integration tests use InMemory so they cannot exercise the automatic fingerprint-driven stale detection path end-to-end. Convex unit tests do exercise it.
nonblockingFindings.spec: tech-design.md:335-342 — ready vs stale is determined by fingerprint comparison at read time. tech-design-server.md:303 — stale means environment exists but canonical inputs no longer match the fingerprint.

nonblockingFindings.title: Four Zod defaults on spec-required fields (pre-acknowledged in addendum)
nonblockingFindings.evidence: Four .default() calls on fields the spec marks as required: lastCheckpointResult (default null), controls (default defaultProcessSurfaceControls), hasEnvironment (default false), accessMode (default 'read_only'). If the server omits these fields from a response, the schema silently fills defaults rather than surfacing a contract violation. Production code always sets these fields explicitly so no behavioral bug exists currently.
nonblockingFindings.spec: Flagged in implementation-addendum.md as non-blocking future cleanup. Epic spec marks these fields as required (lastCheckpointResult: yes | present; controls: yes; hasEnvironment: yes; accessMode: yes).

nonblockingFindings.title: Response composer gated on availableActions not process.controls
nonblockingFindings.evidence: Page gates the respond text-input composer on activeProcess.availableActions.includes('respond') rather than deriving from process.controls. The respond button itself is correctly rendered via renderProcessControls(process.controls). The availableActions check is used only for the optional secondary input surface.
nonblockingFindings.spec: tech-design-client.md:99-103 — client must stop rendering action buttons from availableActions and render from controls. The composer is not a button but the principle extends to all rendering gated on process state.

nonblockingFindings.title: providerKind schema allows null but spec shows only daytona | local
nonblockingFindings.evidence: processEnvironmentStatesTableFields declares providerKind as v.union(v.literal('daytona'), v.literal('local'), v.null()). setProcessHydrationPlan inserts a new row with providerKind: null when no environment exists yet. The orchestrator always sets a real providerKind before any lifecycle work, so null is only a transient initialization state.
nonblockingFindings.spec: tech-design-server.md:368-379 shows providerKind as v.union(v.literal('daytona'), v.literal('local')) without null. The addendum's architecture section does not explicitly address this null addition.

nonblockingFindings.title: buildAdapterHydrationPlan passes empty fingerprint string to adapter
nonblockingFindings.evidence: buildAdapterHydrationPlan sets fingerprint: '' in the HydrationPlan passed to adapters. The adapter echoes it back in HydrationResult. The durable workingSetFingerprint is stored correctly via computeWorkingSetFingerprint called inside upsertProcessEnvironmentState — it is never derived from the plan fingerprint. The empty plan fingerprint is a dead field on the plan shape.
nonblockingFindings.spec: tech-design-server.md:509-533 — HydrationPlan.fingerprint is spec'd as a stable JSON-SHA256 digest. The intent was for the plan to carry the fingerprint so the adapter can echo it back as confirmation.

nonblockingFindings.title: Checkpoint guard condition is inconsistent between outer and inner check
nonblockingFindings.evidence: executeExecution fires runCheckpointAsync when checkpointPlanner !== undefined || codeCheckpointWriter !== undefined || artifactCheckpointPersistence !== platformStore. But runCheckpointAsync itself bails silently if checkpointPlanner === undefined || codeCheckpointWriter === undefined. If exactly one of the two is defined, the outer condition fires but the inner bails silently. In production both are always set together so this edge case is never hit.
nonblockingFindings.spec: No explicit spec rule. Internal implementation consistency issue.

nonblockingFindings.title: ENV_STATES_WITH_ENVIRONMENT omits unavailable state
nonblockingFindings.evidence: ENV_STATES_WITH_ENVIRONMENT does not include 'unavailable'. When environment state becomes 'unavailable', deriveHasEnvironment returns false, so processes.hasEnvironment is set to false. It is unclear from the spec whether unavailable means a working environment may still exist but be unreachable (hasEnvironment = true) or that no environment is present (hasEnvironment = false).
nonblockingFindings.spec: epic.md:137 — unavailable is a distinct environment state. tech-design-server.md:304 — unavailable means environment lifecycle service/provider path is currently unavailable. Spec does not explicitly state hasEnvironment semantics for unavailable.
